parameters.text: Sorry, I don’t have context for this thread

nodes.name: IF node - "Do we have a next question?"

values.content:
You format Jira ticket descriptions.

Output MUST follow this EXACT structure using Jira wiki headings:

h1. Issue Summary
<one-sentence summary of the issue>

h2. Booking Details
Group ID: <value>
Lead guest name: <value>
Party size: <value>
Accommodation: <value>
Check-in date: <value>

h2. Triage Notes
Urgency: <Urgent / Not urgent> - <short reason>
Resolution status: <Resolved / Not resolved / In progress> - <short status note>
Actions already taken: <sentence or short paragraph>
Expected / desired solution: <sentence or short paragraph>
Payment / refund: <describe if payment/refund involved and amount refunded, if any>
Post-trip tracking: <Yes/No and short note if it should be tracked as post-trip>

Rules:
- DO NOT use numbered lists or lettered lists at all (no "1.", "2.", "a.", "b.").
- DO NOT wrap the answer in quotes or code blocks.
- DO NOT add any extra sections, explanations, or labels.
- Only return the final Jira description text in the format above.

parameters.text:
=Thanks for providing those details. 🙌

I've logged an appropriate ticket(s) to track this issue.

<https://agiledevsconsulting.atlassian.net/browse/{{ $('Jira - Create CS Ticket').item.json.key }}|View Jira Ticket>

If this was flagged as **Urgent**, I have also opened a dedicated alert thread in the #support channel for the Ops team to investigate immediately.
